summary: Iteration 2 backend + frontend testing complete. Added 8 new pytest tests covering SSE streaming (EN/MS/ZH + invalid token) and interventions endpoint (teacher/admin allowed, student 403, not-found 404). All 27 backend tests pass in ~16s. Frontend: student lesson page renders with Print/Speaker/Mic/Chat controls all wired and clickable; SSE streaming tutor chat works — assistant bubble streamed a 287-char multi-sentence reply about fractions. Teacher dashboard shows 'AI INTERVENTION' button on each student row; clicking opens interv-modal which loaded summary + 3 recommendations bullets (Groq advice in ~1s); interv-close closes modal cleanly. No JS/page errors.
critical_code_review_comments: tutor/chat/stream passes JWT in URL query string — readable in server access logs and browser history. Acceptable tradeoff for EventSource (which can't set Authorization header), but consider rotating short-lived stream tokens.
EventSource in Lesson.jsx has no manual abort on unmount — navigating away mid-stream leaves an open SSE connection until the backend finishes. Add useEffect cleanup that calls es.close().
interventions endpoint has no caching — every modal open re-calls Groq (~3-10s typical, 1s observed). Consider caching advice per student for a short TTL.
Frontend stores JWT in localStorage; token also leaks into EventSource URL — XSS here would fully compromise the session.
Print CSS relies on .no-print class which is present on interactive UI chrome — good; however the lesson-print area has no @page size directive — default A4 may be fine.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded users + curriculum. New-iteration tests added one TEST_<uuid> user via register (from iter 1 test reused).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login button is rendered with CSS text-transform:uppercase; use selector button:has-text('Log in') (mixed-case) with .last, NOT get_by_role('button', name='LOG IN'). Landing page already shows the login form on the right — no need to click a separate 'Log in' entry. Student topic buttons still have no data-testid but match on title text (Fractions Basics / Photosynthesis / Simple Past Tense). For SSE test in pytest, using requests.get(stream=True) + iter_lines works; parsing 'event:' + 'data:' lines is enough. Intervention modal loads fast (~1s) because Groq responds quickly; still allow 25s in case of cold-start.
